circulars.title: Categorization and Rationalization of Mutual Fund Schemes

sections.heading: 1. Background
sections.content: In order to bring uniformity in the practice of mutual fund schemes and to standardize the scheme categories, SEBI vide circular SEBI/HO/IMD/DF3/CIR/P/2017/114 dated October 06, 2017 prescribed categories of mutual fund schemes. The said circular was further amended by SEBI/HO/IMD/DF3/CIR/P/2018/135 dated December 12, 2018. Based on representations received from AMFI and various stakeholders, it has been decided to further rationalize certain provisions.

sections.heading: 3. Investment Restrictions
sections.content: No mutual fund scheme shall invest more than 10% of its NAV in debt instruments issued by a single issuer. The limit may be extended to 12% with prior approval of the Board of Trustees. For Government Securities, this limit shall not apply. Investment in another scheme under the same AMC or any other mutual fund shall not exceed 5% of the NAV of the scheme.

circulars.title: Categorization and Rationalization of the Schemes - Original Circular

sections.heading: 1. Objective
sections.content: In order to bring uniformity in practice across mutual funds and to standardize the scheme categories and characteristics, the following guidelines are issued. Each mutual fund shall have only one scheme per category, except for Index Funds/ETFs, Fund of Funds, and Sectoral/Thematic Funds.

sections.heading: 2. Definitions
sections.content: Large Cap: 1st-100th company in terms of full market capitalization. Mid Cap: 101st-250th company in terms of full market capitalization. Small Cap: 251st company onwards in terms of full market capitalization. The list of stocks shall be prepared by AMFI and updated semi-annually based on data from BSE and NSE.

circulars.title: Priority Sector Lending - Targets and Classification

sections.heading: 1. Overall Target
sections.content: Domestic scheduled commercial banks (excluding Regional Rural Banks and Small Finance Banks) and foreign banks with 20 and above branches shall maintain priority sector lending at 40% of Adjusted Net Bank Credit (ANBC) or Credit Equivalent Amount of Off-Balance Sheet Exposure, whichever is higher.

rows: Domestic banks & foreign banks with 20+ branches

evaluation_qa.question: What is the minimum equity allocation for a Multi Cap mutual fund scheme?
evaluation_qa.expected_answer: 75% minimum equity allocation as per the revised 2023 circular (SEBI/HO/IMD/IMD-I/DOF5/P/CIR/2023/012). Note: The original 2017 circular required only 65% with 25% each in large/mid/small.
evaluation_qa.reasoning: Tests ability to retrieve current regulation and note amendment from original

evaluation_qa.question: What is the investment limit for a mutual fund scheme in debt instruments of a single issuer?
evaluation_qa.expected_answer: Maximum 10% of NAV, extendable to 12% with Board of Trustees approval. Government Securities are exempt from this limit.
evaluation_qa.reasoning: Tests extraction of numerical limits with exceptions

evaluation_qa.question: What is the definition of a Mid Cap stock according to SEBI?
evaluation_qa.expected_answer: 101st to 250th company in terms of full market capitalization, as per the list prepared by AMFI and updated semi-annually.
evaluation_qa.reasoning: Tests cross-referencing definitions from the original circular when the amending circular references it

evaluation_qa.question: What is the priority sector lending target for agriculture by domestic banks?
evaluation_qa.reasoning: Tests extraction from RBI circular tables

evaluation_qa.question: What is the housing loan limit for priority sector classification in metropolitan centres?
evaluation_qa.expected_answer: Loan limit of INR 35 lakh with property value up to INR 45 lakh, for metropolitan centres with population above 10 lakh.
evaluation_qa.reasoning: Tests table extraction with location-based conditions

evaluation_qa.question: Which circulars does SEBI/HO/IMD/IMD-I/DOF5/P/CIR/2023/012 amend?
evaluation_qa.reasoning: Tests amendment chain tracking - critical for regulatory RAG

evaluation_qa.question: By when must AMCs complete portfolio rebalancing for non-compliant schemes under the 2023 categorization circular?
evaluation_qa.reasoning: Tests extraction of compliance deadlines from tables

evaluation_qa.question: What are the benchmark indices for Large Cap and Flexi Cap mutual fund categories?
evaluation_qa.expected_answer: Large Cap: NIFTY 100 TRI. Flexi Cap: NIFTY 500 TRI.
evaluation_qa.reasoning: Tests multi-row table extraction for comparison queries
